goal_summary: Get Bruna onto a scheduled phone call with Andre by confirming her availability and the remaining logistics after she opened the initial quote and replied she’ll look shortly.
lead_state_summary: Bruna Andrade (Maybe) requested bar service via website and then engaged over SMS. On 05/04 she was live-chatting, and on 05/04 Andre briefly called. On 05/04/23:55 there is an outbound email sending initial quote for bar + food for 50. On 05/05 14:46 Bruna replied in SMS (Portuguese): she hasn’t had time yet to review, and she’ll look when she has a moment. On 05/05 15:02 Andre sent an SMS saying he’s waiting and if she needs, she can call. Known: event area is within immediate area; bar service type is CBO; Andre is the assigned owner (cf_xF8F... indicates Andre). Known contact: email bgrade80@gmail.com and phone +17742448237.
reasoning_trail: Bruna replied that she hasn’t had time to review yet, implying interest but low immediate commitment.
Primary goal is scheduled_call (not tasting/quote) because we already sent quote; next step is availability + decision logistics to move to a call with Andre.

Sequence starts with a low-friction email/SMS that asks for a specific time to talk, then follows with two concrete scheduling options and a single logistics check (service time/address confirmation) to make the call valuable.
Risk accepted: no new broad info; we avoid sending more quote details since she hasn’t had time to review.
We’re hoping to learn Bruna’s next best window to review and confirm bar-service logistics so Andre can recommend the right setup.
known_facts: Lead requested bar service via website (free tasting was requested earlier but current action focus is bar service).
Andre sent an initial quote email for bar + food for 50 on 05/04 22:55 and also sent SMS on 05/04 22:52 referencing email with two directions.
Bruna SMS reply on 05/05 14:46: she hasn’t had time to look yet; will review when she can.
Bruna provided/has phone: +17742448237 and email: bgrade80@gmail.com.
Custom fields indicate CBO bar service and “within immediate area” (05. Within immediate area).
Andre has already been assigned as owner.
unknowns: Event date/time (beyond cf_FV2xBkv... which is 2026-05-23T15:00:00+00:00, but it’s not explicitly confirmed as the event’s exact service time in the conversation text).
Guest count range (quote mentioned 50, but lead’s real plan fit is not confirmed).
Preferred bar service format (staffed vs other) details for CBO are not clarified in message text.
Bruna’s best time window for Andre’s call (only “when I have time” was provided).
best_next_question: Qual horário fica melhor pra você pra eu passar as opções com o Andre e confirmar os detalhes do bar — hoje à tarde (15–17) ou amanhã (10–12)?

days.objective: Convert Bruna’s “I’ll look later” reply into a specific phone-call time with Andre.
days.reasoning: She already indicated she will review when she has time, so the fastest next step is to offer two concrete call windows and use SMS/phone availability to reduce effort.

required_actions.intent: Schedule a phone call with Andre
required_actions.draft_seed: Oi Bruna! Entendi — quando você puder olhar a cotação. Pra eu te ajudar mais rápido com o Andre, qual horário fica melhor: hoje 15–17 ou amanhã 10–12 (horário local)?
required_actions.notes: Uses her Portuguese reply to offer an easy yes choice with time ranges, aiming to secure a scheduled call and avoid sending any new broad info.

days.objective: If no scheduling reply, confirm one key logistics item to make the upcoming call immediately useful.
days.reasoning: If she didn’t answer the time question, we should keep momentum by asking a simple logistics check (venue/address + service timing) that Andre can use in the call.

required_actions.intent: Request venue/address and confirm service time before scheduling
required_actions.draft_seed: Bruna, tudo bem? Pra eu alinhar certinho com o Andre, pode me dizer só: qual é o endereço (ou cidade/bairro) e que horário vocês esperam que o bar comece/termine? Com isso eu te indico o melhor formato.
required_actions.notes: Email is appropriate since a quote thread exists; it asks for two concrete logistics items that typically unblock bar-service recommendations.

days.objective: Close the scheduling loop with a binary, low-pressure close that still routes to a call.
days.reasoning: After an email without a confirmed time, use a smaller ask: either schedule a call or confirm she needs more time; this reduces pressure and respects that she hasn’t reviewed yet.

required_actions.draft_seed: Bruna, você prefere que o Andre te ligue pra ajustar os detalhes (sim) ou quer que eu te mande 1 pergunta bem rápida por aqui primeiro (não)?
required_actions.notes: A NEPQ-style binary choice lowers friction and helps determine whether to push for scheduling or gather one quick missing detail.

days.objective: Offer to use the live-chat link for context or reconfirm the phone-call availability.
days.reasoning: Because there is a Crisp live chat link in the profile field, an operator task can check if there’s any extra context (or confirm contact details) and unblock scheduling without spamming.

required_actions.intent: Check Crisp chat for latest buyer needs / best contact context
required_actions.draft_seed: Use the lead’s Crisp profile link (cf_smf4xXJPUNl...) to review the last messages/context and confirm Bruna’s preferred contact method or any missing details to prep Andre’s call.
required_actions.notes: This aligns with existing live chat activity and helps us avoid guessing; it’s a safe internal action that supports a faster scheduled call.

required_actions.intent: Schedule call using fallback time options
required_actions.draft_seed: Bruna, só pra facilitar: o Andre consegue te atender hoje 16–18 ou amanhã 11–13. Qual desses encaixa melhor?
required_actions.notes: Provides fresh, concrete fallback windows to convert intent into a scheduled call after earlier “when I have time” messaging.

days.objective: Fit-check the quote direction so the call has a clear purpose (without defending price).
days.reasoning: She hasn’t reviewed the quote yet; a fit-check question is smaller than another logistics dump and makes Andre’s call more targeted once she confirms what she wants.

required_actions.intent: Fit-check question about bar setup scope
required_actions.draft_seed: Bruna, quando você olhar a cotação, me confirma só uma coisa: vocês precisam do serviço de bar apenas, ou bar + comida também (do jeito da proposta)? Assim o Andre já chega com o setup certo na ligação.
required_actions.notes: Avoids broad re-sending and asks a single fit clarification that directly determines what Andre should recommend on the call.

days.objective: If still no response, propose scheduling by asking for the earliest time she can talk.
days.reasoning: When a lead remains silent, the lowest-friction scheduling ask is to ask for the earliest slot she can offer rather than repeating multiple options.

required_actions.intent: Request earliest available time to talk
required_actions.draft_seed: Bruna, pra eu agendar com o Andre sem te atrapalhar: qual é o primeiro horário que você consegue falar essa semana?
required_actions.notes: Direct, low-pressure scheduling prompt; NEPQ goal is to get a specific answer that enables a scheduled call.

days.objective: Final attempt to schedule: confirm call vs. pause, and stop if she declines.
days.reasoning: By day 7, multiple touches without a scheduled call warrants a respectful close that either books the call or confirms she’s not ready, preventing ongoing outreach if she’s not interested.

required_actions.intent: Request explicit next step (schedule call or pause)
required_actions.draft_seed: Bruna, só me diz qual caminho prefere: (1) agendar uma ligação rápida com o Andre pra alinharmos o bar, ou (2) se preferir, eu aguardo você olhar a proposta e retomo depois. O que funciona melhor pra você?
required_actions.notes: Asks for an explicit next step (schedule vs. wait) with minimal commitment, reducing the chance of dead-end silence.

stop_conditions.trigger: Lead replies with a scheduled time for Andre or confirms call booked
